activities_projects.title: Verification of Mirror Formula for Concave Mirror
activities_projects.objective: Verify mirror formula and magnification using concave mirror
steps: Set up concave mirror on optical bench
Fix object at different positions (beyond C, at C, between C & F)
Adjust screen for sharp real image
Record u, v, calculate f using 1/f = 1/v + 1/u, and magnification m = -v/u
Tabulate 5-6 readings and find average f
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Verifies mirror formula experimentally and understands sign convention
activities_projects.evaluation: Table accuracy, calculations, and average focal length (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Ray Diagram Construction Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Draw precise ray diagrams for spherical mirrors and lenses
steps: Draw ray diagrams for concave mirror (object between F & P, at infinity)
Draw for convex lens (object at 2F, beyond 2F)
Use at least 3 rays each, label nature, position, size
Compare with experimental observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters ray construction rules and predicts image characteristics

activities_projects.title: Refractive Index of Glass Slab with Pins
activities_projects.objective: Determine refractive index using pins and lateral shift
steps: Fix slab, draw outline
Place two pins for incident ray at 30°, 45°, 60°
Look through slab, place emergent pins in line
Trace rays, measure i, r, lateral shift d
Calculate μ = sin i / sin r and from shift formula
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Calculates refractive index accurately from multiple angles
activities_projects.evaluation: Graph of sin i vs sin r, average μ (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Power of Convex Lens Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Find focal length and power of convex lens using distant object
steps: Focus image of distant object on screen
Measure image distance ≈ f
Repeat 5 times for different positions
Calculate power P = 1/f (in m)
Discuss errors
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands lens power and its unit dioptre
activities_projects.evaluation: Readings table, average P, error discussion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparison of Mirrors and Lenses Applications
activities_projects.objective: Analyze real-life uses with ray diagrams
steps: Select 4 devices: shaving mirror, rear-view mirror, magnifying glass, projector lens
Draw ray diagrams showing image formation
Explain why specific mirror/lens is used
Discuss advantages/disadvantages
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies concepts to practical optical instruments

activities_projects.title: Total Internal Reflection Demonstration
activities_projects.objective: Observe TIR using laser and water stream or prism
materials: Transparent plastic bottle with water hole
steps: Fill bottle, poke small hole, shine laser into stream
Observe light bending and staying in stream (TIR)
Explain critical angle and conditions for TIR
Relate to optical fibres

activities_projects.title: Lens Combination Activity
activities_projects.objective: Study effective focal length of two thin lenses in contact
steps: Find individual f1, f2 using distant object
Place in contact, find combined f
Verify 1/F = 1/f1 + 1/f2
Calculate power addition
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns lens power addition for combination

activities_projects.title: Apparent Depth vs Real Depth Graph
activities_projects.objective: Plot graph and find refractive index of water
steps: Vary water depth, measure real and apparent depth
Take 6-8 readings
Plot real vs apparent depth, find slope = μ
Discuss limitations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Uses graphical method for refractive index
activities_projects.evaluation: Graph accuracy, μ value, discussion (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sign Convention Application Numerical Poster
steps: Collect 5 numerical problems (mirror/lens formula, magnification, power)
Solve step-by-step with sign convention
Present on poster with diagrams
Explain common mistakes
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Masters numerical solving with sign rules

activities_projects.title: Optical Illusion Model with Mirrors
steps: Fix mirrors at angle to create multiple images
Place object, observe infinite images
Explain using laws of reflection
Relate to kaleidoscope
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies reflection to create optical effects
